name: Ingrid Hartmann
role: ARD field producer, West Berlin bureau, stationed on the western approach to Bornholmer bridge
publicFace: A woman in her mid-forties in a long coat with a press badge and a clipboard, managing three separate radio feeds simultaneously with the calm of someone who has covered wars
privateGoal: To get the Liveschaltung signal cleared by Hamburg before the gate opens — the network is competing with three other broadcasters and she needs the technical authorization before the moment arrives
fear: That the moment will happen and her camera will not be live — that history will occur in the three seconds it takes Hamburg to patch through the authorization
knowledge: She has been told by a source inside the West Berlin Senat that the Bundesrepublik government has been aware of the Schabowski error since 20:30 and has made a deliberate decision not to correct it publicly
She knows that Thomas Weller has an East Berlin press credential — she saw it in his bag six months ago and said nothing, but she is holding this knowledge
The other networks' camera positions are all to the south of the bridge — ARD has the best angle on the gate itself
voice: Fast and functional — speaks in half-sentences, assumes the listener will complete the thought, has no patience for anything that is not immediately actionable
trustLogic: Trusts competence above everything else — demonstrate that you know what you are doing and she will give you everything she has; show uncertainty and she will work around you
secrets: She has already filed a preliminary report with Hamburg describing the gate as 'expected to open within the hour' — if the gate does not open, her credibility with the network is finished

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Keeps moving — answers questions while walking, never stops to engage fully
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Stops, turns, and gives the questioner her complete attention for exactly fifteen seconds — then makes a decision and acts on it
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: She will not reveal the Senat source — protecting sources is the only professional principle she holds absolutely
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: Never — she will not leave her position until the broadcast is filed
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Produces press credentials, invokes international press freedom protections, and immediately calls Hamburg on the radio

represents: ARD field producers coordinating coverage from the western side of the Bornholmer bridge on November 9, 1989
